purpose: Keep the domain taxonomy subsystem thin, routed and split by boundary across taxonomy registry, human guides, refinement loop state, visual views and audit outputs.
principle: The domain taxonomy follows the root AGENTS.md pattern: the seed file is a thin entrypoint, the registry JSON is classification truth, loop files own refinement state, and detailed human explanations are routed through this docs map.

baseline_doc.role: Thin human entrypoint for the domain taxonomy subsystem
not_for: Full level-1 and level-2 baseline table

machine_truth.role: Canonical level-1, level-2 and level-3 taxonomy registry
Registry for reusable loops, including taxonomy-refinement
Taxonomy refinement loop method and quality gates
Machine contract for loop commands and run records
Canonical map for domain taxonomy documentation and loop asset boundaries

doc_routes.boundary: Only explains where to start, which files own which topic and which machine truth decides classification.

doc_routes.role: Human baseline for taxonomy layers, fields, status and level-1/level-2 structure
doc_routes.boundary: Explains the stable first-pass taxonomy baseline for humans.

doc_routes.role: Human-readable level-3 taxonomy overview derived from registry
doc_routes.boundary: Presents the current full three-level taxonomy for browsing.

doc_routes.role: Human guide for assigning primary and related taxonomy categories
doc_routes.boundary: Explains routing order, decision rules, examples and quality gates.

doc_routes.role: Obsidian visual overview of the taxonomy structure
doc_routes.boundary: Visual navigation and inspection surface for the taxonomy.

doc_routes.role: Obsidian visual matrix for the complete level-2 and level-3 taxonomy
doc_routes.boundary: Visual drilldown surface that displays all 45 level-2 groups and 450 level-3 categories derived from the registry.

doc_routes.role: Thin entrypoint for taxonomy-refinement loop assets
doc_routes.boundary: Only explains loop file ownership and read order.

doc_routes.role: Human index of completed refinement summaries
doc_routes.boundary: Indexes loop output summaries generated by completed units.

doc_routes.role: Taxonomy routing and coverage evaluation notes
doc_routes.boundary: Records quality checks and audit conclusions for taxonomy routing.

split_policy.baseline_doc_rule: domain-taxonomy-seed.md stays thin. Move status, tables, routing rules and loop details to their owning routed files.
split_policy.machine_truth_rule: Classification truth lives in domain-taxonomy.registry.json. Markdown may summarize or explain but must not override registry data.
split_policy.loop_truth_rule: Refinement state lives in state.json and run records; outputs/*.md are human summaries.
split_policy.growth_trigger: When taxonomy documentation adds a new durable topic, create a new routed file and register it here before relying on it.

visual_coverage.rule: The domain taxonomy must have both a high-level overview Canvas and a full level-3 matrix Canvas because primary_level_3 is the required routing unit for knowledge entries.

required_views.coverage: level-1 and level-2 overview plus routing quality gates
complete 45 level-2 groups and 450 level-3 categories

visual_coverage.validation_gate: scripts/validate-system.ps1 checks that required taxonomy visual views exist and are registered in system/canvas-registry.json.
